text: By the year 2000, the United Kingdom will be simply a small group of impoverished islands, inhabited by some 70 million hungry people. If I were a gambler, I would take even money that England will not exist in the year 2000.
author: Paul Ehrlich
source: Speech at British Institute for Biology, 1971

didItHoldUp.analysis: England very much still exists. By 2000, the UK had the world's fourth-largest economy, its citizens were among the best-fed on Earth, and London was solidifying its position as a global financial capital. Ehrlich's apocalyptic visions consistently mistook real environmental concerns for imminent civilizational collapse. The underlying worry about resource limits wasn't foolish — but the catastrophism was so extreme that it discredited the message. When you predict a country will cease to exist and it thrives instead, people stop listening.
